description_long: This object is a cluster of large, angular stone blocks arranged in a staggered, overlapping formation. The blocks have a rough, chiseled appearance with irregular edges and flat surfaces, suggesting they are hewn from natural stone. The coloration is predominantly a pale, weathered gray with subtle variations and some faint discoloration, possibly from exposure to the elements. The surfaces are matte and slightly textured, with visible signs of wear but no significant cracks or chips. The base of the blocks appears to be in contact with a thin layer of grass or earth, indicating outdoor placement. The overall form is low and wide, making it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A cluster of large, pale gray stone blocks with rough, chiseled surfaces and irregular, overlapping arrangement. The blocks are weathered and rest on a grassy base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the angular, overlapping arrangement of the stone blocks with visible surface texture and some discoloration.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the stepped, layered profile and the thickness of each block.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the broad, flat surfaces and the overall width of the stone cluster.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the slanted, stacked nature and the depth of the blocks.
attribute_location_context: urban park landscaping or public plaza barrier
attribute_installation_method: set directly on compacted soil or grass
attribute_weathering_pattern: Some faint discoloration and surface wear, likely from exposure to weather and foot traffic. No major cracks or chips are visible.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: clustered together as a freestanding barrier or obstacle
